threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a ‘roundabout’ instead of ‘the junction’ and avoid the cafes that only serve oat milk. Classic sign you're fresh off the plane.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week. A couple argued over who was more 'punk' while the driver blasted 80s hits. True South London vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw three generations arguing over the best döner at the corner shop while the local cats lounged in the sun, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a kebab shop that doubles as a gallery, showcasing local artists alongside your döner.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know the best taco truck changes its salsa every week, but tourists stick with the same old burrito spot every time.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: I once saw a guy playing the sax on the 14-L, totally lost in his own world. No one seemed to care, just another day on Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just about the parks and plazas. They miss the local markets where the real flavors of the neighborhood hide.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fascinated by the street performers at Plaça del Sol, snapping pics while the locals just stroll by.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cringe at the idea of ordering a 'half' pint at the pub, not realizing that's just how we roll around here.
Last night, a guy on the 47 was talking to an empty seat like it was his best mate. The driver chuckled and said, 'Ain't we all there sometimes?'

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy holding a yoga mat under one arm and a kebab in the other, I realized Neukölln's charm lies in its perfect chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a kebab shop open 24/7 blasting techno—where the line at 3 AM feels like a mini-festival.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the best tacos are at La Taqueria after 11 p.m. when the street lamps flicker like a secret code.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus at 1 a.m. last week, driver blasting old-school hip hop. Riders started vibing; felt like a mini block party on wheels.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the plaza is just for photos, missing that locals gather to gossip and share tapas almost every evening.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer in Gràcia by the way they insist on addressing the metro as 'subway' instead of 'metro'.
